headline: Model Context Protocol and agent latency optimization dominated the week as tool-use plumbing broke out across Google Cloud demos.
body: Model Context Protocol led the window, positioned as the tool-discovery layer that lets agents chain tools and reason about selection where traditional APIs cannot, and it anchored an enterprise agent demo orchestrating Oracle, Looker, and vector stores. Alongside it, parallel execution, profiling, and bottleneck architecture broke out through two Google Cloud case studies cutting agent execution time by 80% via workflow parallelization, OpenTelemetry instrumentation, and Antigravity IDE profiling. On the model side, Sonnet 5 shipped but drew a review calling it an underperformer against GLM-5.2 at a higher price, while Kestra surfaced as a YAML-based Airflow alternative. Separately, OpenAI framed strategy around merging chatbots with agents and warned of narrowing human oversight as models move toward self-sustaining research loops.
